codeReview.summary: The feature delivers the page, matrix UI, filters, reverse file view, and nav entry, but it fails a core requirement: GitHub links are not reliably constructed from git remote data due to a response-shape mismatch when reading /api/project.

issues.description: fetchGitRemote reads `json.data.git_remote` / `json.data.github_url`, but `/api/project` returns these under `json.data.project` (see lib/bropilot/api/handlers/project.ex:57). Result: `gitHubBaseUrl` remains null in normal flows, so file paths render as plain text instead of clickable GitHub URLs, violating expected behavior.
GitHub links are hardcoded to `/blob/main/` instead of deriving branch/ref. Repositories using a non-`main` default branch will produce broken links.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Frontend fetch logic was duplicated in the page instead of consistently using API client abstractions, which likely contributed to the contract mismatch bug.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: AGENTS.md:34 says to import API functions from web/src/lib/api.ts and not duplicate fetch logic; traceability.astro implements its own getApiConfig + raw fetch calls at lines 460-478 and 431-432.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The astro-frontend skill asks for Vitest tests when configured, but this implementation added substantial Alpine logic without adding new tests; guidance may need clarification on when tests are mandatory for interactive pages.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/astro-frontend/SKILL.md line 37 requires writing Vitest tests if configured; handoff for session e2a27b9c-fa27-406e-98c2-29f3324a913a reports no tests added.

summary: Review failed due to a blocking GitHub URL construction defect: the page reads /api/project with the wrong response path, so clickable GitHub links are typically not produced. A secondary non-blocking issue is hardcoded `main` branch URLs.
